pathwayTagline: Protect → observe → move → restore → clean → feed → eliminate

sections.title: Infection Control
sections.rationale: Every clinical skill begins and ends with infection control. Students must internalize this as muscle memory before touching a patient.

sections.title: Measurement and Recording
sections.rationale: Observation-only skills with no physical intervention. They train precision and documentation before students are trusted to act.

sections.title: Mobility and Positioning
sections.rationale: Before personal care, students must move patients safely. Positioning and transfers prevent injury during bathing, dressing, and elimination.

sections.title: Restorative and Support
sections.rationale: Once mobility is established, students learn to maintain and restore function — preventing deterioration, not just assisting movement.

sections.title: Personal Care
sections.rationale: The core of daily CNA work. Mobility skills are prerequisites for bathing, grooming, and dressing with dignity.

sections.title: Feeding and Nutrition
sections.rationale: Feeding follows hygiene and positioning. It is cognitively distinct — swallowing observation, aspiration risk, and dignity at the table.

sections.title: Elimination and Peri Care
sections.rationale: The most intimate care. Full confidence in infection control, mobility, and personal care comes first — then elimination.

skills.title: Hand Hygiene (Hand Washing)
skills.pedagogicalReason: The single universal act. Teaches the before-and-after mindset — nothing happens without it.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself and identify the patient.
Turn on the water and adjust the flow so that the water is warm.

steps.note: Avoid splashing on uniform; keep forearms and wrists lower than elbows.
steps.detailedText: Wet hands thoroughly, keeping hands and forearms lower than elbows. Avoid splashing water on uniform.
Apply a palm-sized amount of hand soap.

subSteps: Rub hands palm to palm
Rub back of right and left hand (fingers interlaced)
Rub palm to palm with fingers interlaced
Perform rotational rubbing of left and right thumbs
Rub fingertips against palm of opposite hand
Rub wrists
Repeat sequence at least two times
steps.detailedText: Perform hand hygiene using plenty of lather and friction for at least 20 seconds.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Lather all surfaces ≥20 sec; repeat full sequence ≥2×

steps.note: Credentia absorbs this into Steps 5 & 7 — GWC scores it as a standalone observable action. Students must demonstrate it explicitly.
steps.detailedText: Keep fingertips pointing downward throughout the entire handwashing process.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Fingertips down throughout entire wash

subSteps: Rub fingertips against palm of opposite hand
steps.detailedText: Clean under fingernails.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Clean under all fingernails

steps.detailedText: Rinse hands with water, keeping fingertips pointing down so water runs off fingertips. Do not shake water from your hands.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Rinse fingertips-down; do not shake water from hands

steps.detailedText: Dry hands thoroughly from fingers to wrists with a paper towel or air dryer. Dispose of the paper towel(s).
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Dry fingertip-to-wrist; use paper towel or air dryer

steps.note: GWC: paper towel only. Credentia also allows knee/foot control — GWC governs on exam.
steps.detailedText: Use a new paper towel to turn off the water. Dispose of the paper towel.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Turn off faucet with clean paper towel; dispose immediately

steps.text: Never lean on or touch sink
steps.detailedText: Do not lean against the sink or touch the inside of the sink during the handwashing process.
steps.examScorecard: Safety: No contact with sink at any time (lean or touch)

skills.studentFocus: First skill on many CA exam lists; required before all patient contact.

skills.title: Donning and Removing PPE (Gown and Gloves)
skills.pedagogicalReason: Builds on hand hygiene: wash before donning and after removing. The sequence is the lesson.

steps.detailedText: Face the back opening of the gown.
Unfold the gown.
Put arms into the sleeves.
Secure the neck opening at the back of neck.
Secure the waist, making sure that the back flaps overlap each other and cover clothing as completely as possible.

steps.note: Use clean, non-sterile gloves for isolation care.
steps.detailedText: Don clean, non-sterile gloves.
Ensure the gloves overlap the gown sleeves at the wrist.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Gloves overlap gown cuffs at wrist

steps.note: Sequence matters: gloves off before gown, before leaving the room — evaluator watches for this order.
steps.detailedText: When the care is complete and before leaving the room, remove the gloves BEFORE removing the gown.
steps.examScorecard: Safety: Remove gloves BEFORE gown; BEFORE leaving room

subSteps: Grasp outside of first glove at palm; peel off
Slide fingers of bare hand under cuff of remaining glove at wrist; peel off turning inside out
steps.detailedText: Remove the gloves, turning them inside out.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Both gloves removed inside out

steps.detailedText: Dispose of the gloves in the appropriate container.
steps.examScorecard: Safety: Dispose without touching outer glove surface

steps.note: ⚠️ VIDEO WARNING: This step is missing from the reference video — video performs hand hygiene only after gown disposal. GWC requires it HERE, between glove removal and gown unfastening.
steps.detailedText: Perform hand hygiene.
steps.examScorecard: Safety: Hand hygiene between gloves-off and gown-off

steps.detailedText: Unfasten the gown at the neck.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Unfasten neck before removing gown

steps.detailedText: Unfasten the gown at the waist.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Unfasten waist separately from neck

subSteps: Start at shoulders; pull down
Turn inside out as you remove
Fold soiled area to soiled area
Hold away from body; do not touch floor
steps.detailedText: Remove the gown starting at the top of the shoulders, turning it inside out and folding the soiled area to soiled area.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Remove from shoulders; inside out; soiled-to-soiled

steps.detailedText: Dispose of the gown in an appropriate container.
steps.examScorecard: Safety: Dispose without contaminating self

steps.detailedText: Perform hand hygiene.
steps.examScorecard: Safety: Final hand hygiene after full PPE removal

skills.studentFocus: Isolation skill — no patient intro; gloves off before gown at exit.

skills.title: Counts and Records Radial Pulse
skills.pedagogicalReason: Simplest vital sign — one finger location, one count, one unit (bpm). Introduces the count-then-record loop.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title; explain the procedure clearly, slowly, and directly, maintaining face-to-face contact.
Place the tips of three fingers on the thumb side of the patient's wrist.

steps.note: Start on a whole number on the watch, then count
steps.detailedText: Count beats for a full 60 seconds.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach and confirm the patient can use it.
Perform hand hygiene (20 sec soap and water, or alcohol rub if hands not visibly soiled).
Record the pulse rate accurately.
steps.examScorecard: Tolerance: ±4 beats/min vs evaluator

skills.studentFocus: Full 60 seconds at radial artery; document ±4.

skills.title: Counts and Records Respirations
skills.pedagogicalReason: Taught immediately after pulse because they share a 60-second window. Observe without alerting the patient.

steps.note: Say "for testing purposes." In real practice, never tell the patient you're counting respirations — it changes the rate.
steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title; explain the procedure clearly, slowly, and directly, maintaining face-to-face contact.

steps.note: Start on a whole number on the watch, then count.
steps.detailedText: Observe the rise and fall of the chest for a full 60 seconds.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach and confirm the patient can use it.
Perform hand hygiene (20 sec soap and water, or alcohol rub if hands not visibly soiled).
Record the respiratory rate accurately.
steps.examScorecard: Tolerance: ±4 breaths/min vs evaluator

skills.studentFocus: Do not tell the patient you are counting respirations.

skills.title: Measures and Records Manual Blood Pressure
skills.pedagogicalReason: Most complex vital sign — equipment, two values, and interpretation. Builds on pulse and respiration rhythm.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Cleanse the stethoscope and blood pressure cuff prior to placing it on the patient's skin.

steps.text: Position patient; arm at heart level
steps.note: Ask which arm the patient prefers.
steps.detailedText: Place the patient in a relaxed reclining or sitting position. The patient should be seated quietly for at least five minutes in a chair prior to blood pressure measurement. Ask the patient which arm they prefer to use. Both feet should be on the floor and the arm should be supported at heart level.
Remove or rearrange clothing so the cuff and the stethoscope are on bare skin.

steps.note: Lower cuff margin sits 1″ above the elbow bend.
steps.detailedText: Center the bladder of the blood pressure cuff over the brachial artery with the lower margin 1″ above the antecubital space. Fit the cuff evenly and snugly. Palpate the brachial artery in the antecubital space.

steps.note: If a beat is heard immediately on deflation, fully deflate, wait 1 min, reinflate to no more than 200.
steps.detailedText: Inflate the cuff to 160-180 mmHg.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Inflate cuff 160–180 mmHg

steps.detailedText: Deflate the cuff gradually at a constant rate by opening the valve on the bulb (2-3 mm Hg/second) until the first Korotkoff sound is heard. Note the systolic pressure.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Deflate rate 2–3 mm Hg/second (systolic)

steps.detailedText: Continue to deflate the cuff slowly at 2 mm Hg/second. Note the point at which Korotkoff sounds disappear completely as the diastolic pressure.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Deflate rate 2 mm Hg/second (diastolic)

steps.detailedText: Deflate the cuff completely and remove the cuff from the patient's arm.
Inform the patient of the blood pressure reading.
Cleanse the stethoscope and blood pressure cuff.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

steps.note: Both numbers must each be within ±8 mmHg.
steps.detailedText: Document both systolic and diastolic pressures each within plus or minus 8 mmHg of the evaluator's reading.
steps.examScorecard: Exam tolerance: Document BP ±8 mmHg (systolic AND diastolic)

skills.studentFocus: Cuff on bare skin; cleanse equipment; document ±8 mmHg.

skills.title: Measures and Records Weight of Ambulatory Client
skills.pedagogicalReason: Requires the patient to be upright and cooperative. Bridges measurement toward mobility.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Verify the patient is wearing nonskid footwear.
Balance (or zero) scale.
Walk the patient to the scale.
Assist the patient to step on the scale.
Check that the patient is centered on the scale.
Check that the patient has their arms at their side.
Ensure the patient is not holding on to anything that would alter the reading of the weight.
Adjust the weights until the scale is in balance or read analog scale.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

steps.note: Tolerance: record weight within ±2 lb / 0.9 kg of actual.
steps.detailedText: Document weight (plus/minus 2 lbs. or 0.9 kg).
steps.examScorecard: Exam tolerance: Document weight ±2 lb / 0.9 kg

steps.detailedText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Zero scale; nonskid footwear; document ±2 lbs.

skills.title: Measures and Records Urinary Output
skills.pedagogicalReason: The only measurement tied to a bodily output. Bridge skill into the elimination section.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Don clean, non-sterile gloves.
Pour the liquid in the bedpan into a measuring container.
Rinse the bedpan and empty the water into the toilet.
Measure the amount of urine at eye level with the container on a flat surface.
Empty the urine into the toilet.
Rinse the measuring container with water and empty it into the toilet.

Remove gloves by turning them inside out — grasp the outside of one glove at the wrist and peel it off, then slide the fingers of the ungloved hand under the remaining glove at the wrist and peel it off turning inside out. Dispose of both gloves in the appropriate waste container without touching the outer surface.

steps.note: Tolerance: record urine volume within ±25 mL of actual.
steps.detailedText: Record the volume within plus or minus 25 mL of the actual volume.
steps.examScorecard: Exam tolerance: Record volume ±25 mL

steps.detailedText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Measure in container — not the same as full bedpan skill.

skills.title: Positions on Side
skills.pedagogicalReason: Bed-based, lowest-risk movement. No equipment. Teaches body mechanics, alignment, and patient comfort.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Position the bed flat.
Raise the bed height.

steps.text: Raise far side rail for safety
steps.detailedText: Raise the side rail on the side of the bed the patient will be facing after repositioning for safety.

steps.text: Move to working side of bed
steps.detailedText: Move to the working side of the bed, which is opposite the side rail that was raised.

steps.text: Explain move toward you on three
steps.detailedText: Explain to the patient that the candidate will move them closer to the candidate before turning on the count of three.

steps.text: Count three; move patient toward you
steps.detailedText: From the working side of the bed count to three and move the patient towards you.

steps.text: Instruct patient to move near arm
steps.detailedText: Instruct the patient to move their arm closest to the raised side rail away from their body. If able, the patient should grasp the side rail with the hand closest to you, reaching across their own body.

steps.text: Raise patient's near knee to assist
steps.detailedText: Raise the patient's knee that is closest to the candidate to assist in turning.

steps.text: Explain turning toward rail on three
steps.detailedText: Explain that the candidate will turn the patient towards the side rail on count three.

steps.text: Count three; turn toward side rail
steps.detailedText: Count to three to turn the patient towards the raised side rail.

steps.text: Guard face from rail and pillow
steps.detailedText: Ensure that the patient's face never comes close to the side rail or becomes covered by the pillow.

steps.text: Check patient not on bottom arm
steps.detailedText: Check that the patient is not lying on their bottom arm.
Place a pillow behind the patient's back, ensuring they will not roll back to the supine position.

steps.text: Check body alignment from bed end
steps.detailedText: Move to the end of the bed and check that the patient is in correct body alignment.
Verify that the patient is in the middle of the bed.

steps.text: Pillow under top arm; protect ribs
steps.detailedText: Place a pillow between the patient's top arm and their rib cage or the bed, ensuring the elbow is not directly on their ribs.

steps.text: Pillow under top knee; protect ankles
steps.detailedText: Place a pillow under the top knee, ensuring the knee is not resting directly on the other knee or the ankle is not on top of the other ankle.
Adjust the pillow under the patient's head for comfort.
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Pillows for alignment; face not against side rail.

skills.title: Transfers from Bed to Wheelchair Using Transfer Belt
skills.pedagogicalReason: The patient leaves the bed. Requires belt, coordination, and two-point communication.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Check the brakes on the bed to ensure they are locked.

steps.text: Remove wheelchair foot pedals if needed
steps.detailedText: Remove the foot pedals from the wheelchair if needed.

steps.text: Assist patient to sit; dangle feet
steps.detailedText: Assist the patient to a seated position on the side of the bed with their feet on the floor; allow them to dangle their feet for a few minutes.
Assist the patient in putting on nonskid footwear.
Place the gait belt on the patient.

steps.text: Position wheelchair; strong side toward it
steps.detailedText: Position the wheelchair at the head or foot of the bed so the patient will move towards the wheelchair with the stronger side of their body. The wheelchair should touch the side of the bed.
Lock the brakes on the wheelchair.
Ask the patient if they feel dizzy or light-headed.
Face the patient and place each of feet in front of the patient's feet to prevent them from slipping.
Instruct the patient to push up on the bed to aid in standing on the count of three.
Grasp the gait belt with both hands, with palms and fingertips pointing up.

steps.text: Count three; assist patient to stand
steps.detailedText: Count to three and assist the patient to stand.
Assist the patient to pivot.

steps.text: Instruct patient to grasp wheelchair arms
steps.detailedText: Instruct the patient to grasp the arms of the wheelchair when they can feel the back of their knees are in contact with the wheelchair seat.

steps.text: Assist patient to seated in wheelchair
steps.detailedText: Assist the patient to a seated position in the wheelchair.
Remove the gait belt gently to avoid skin injury.
Release the wheelchair brakes.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Lock bed and wheelchair brakes; ask dizzy; pivot.

skills.title: Assists to Ambulate Using Transfer Belt
skills.pedagogicalReason: Most demanding mobility skill — patient fully upright and moving. Gait, balance, and fall prevention all apply.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Place nonskid footwear on the patient.
Adjust the bed to a safe level.
Check the brakes of the bed to ensure they are locked.
Allow the patient to sit and dangle on the edge of the bed before standing to ambulate.
Ask the patient if they feel dizzy or light-headed.
Properly place the gait belt around the patient's waist and check the gait belt for tightness by slipping fingers between the gait belt and the patient.
Face the patient and place each of the feet in front of the patient's feet to prevent them from slipping.
Instruct the patient to push up on the bed on the count of three to assist with standing.
Count to three and assist the patient to a standing position.

steps.text: Move to weak side; hold belt
steps.detailedText: Move to the weak side of the patient, slightly behind them. Hold the gait belt with palms and fingertips pointing upwards.
Assist the patient in a standing position.
Walk slightly behind the patient for a distance of 10 feet while safely holding the transfer belt.

steps.text: Assist back to bed; remove belt
steps.detailedText: Safely assist the client back to bed and remove the transfer belt.

steps.text: Call light; bed low and locked
steps.detailedText: Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient. Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Nonskid footwear; gait belt; ask about dizziness.

skills.title: Performs Modified PROM for One Shoulder
skills.pedagogicalReason: Upper body first. Larger joint, more intuitive range of motion. Introduces PROM mechanics.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Advise patients to report pain during movement.
Place one hand under the patient's elbow with palm facing up.
Place the other hand under the patient's wrist with palm facing up.
Watch the patient for objective signs of pain during movement.
Move their arms gently and stop if there is any resistance.
While keeping the patient's arm straight, raise their arm up and over their head (i.e., flexion).
Bring the patient's arm back down to their side (i.e., extension).
Complete flexion and extension movements of the shoulder according to the order in the restorative care plan.
Continue to support the elbow and wrist of the patient.
Keeping the patient's arm straight, move their entire arm out away from the body (i.e., abduction).
Move their arms gently and stop if there is any resistance.
Return the patient's arm to their side (adduction).
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Same pain rules as knee/ankle PROM.

skills.title: Performs Modified PROM for One Knee and One Ankle
skills.pedagogicalReason: Lower body, two joints. More complex; builds on the shoulder movement pattern already learned.
skills.rtcVideoTitle: 16 Passive Range of Motion One Knee

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Advise patients to report pain during movement.

subSteps: Keeping the patient's leg straight, gently move their entire leg away from their
body (i.e., abduction).
Move their legs gently and stop if there is any resistance.
Ask the patient if they are experiencing any pain during movement.
Stop the ROM movement if the patient reports pain or displays objective signs
of pain.
Keeping the patient's leg straight, move their entire leg toward their body (i.e.,
adduction).
Complete abduction and adduction movements of the hip according to the
order in their restorative care plan.
Continue to correctly support joints by keeping one hand under the patient's
knee and the other hand under the patient's ankle.
steps.detailedText: Abduction/Adduction for Hip:

subSteps: Bend the patient's knee and hip up toward the patient's trunk (i.e., flexion of
hip and knee at the same time).
Move the patient's leg gently and stop if there is any resistance.
Ask the patient if they are experiencing any pain during movement.
Stop the ROM movement if the patient reports pain or displays objective signs
of pain.
Straighten their knee and hip (i.e., extension of knee and hip at the same time).
Complete flexion and extension movements of the knee and hip according to
the order in the restorative care plan.
steps.detailedText: Flexion/Extension of Knee and Hip:
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Stop ROM if patient reports pain.

skills.title: Applies One Knee-High Elastic Stocking
skills.pedagogicalReason: Applied after PROM — supports the same lower-extremity circulation PROM exercises protect. Same goal, different tool.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Position the patient in the supine position. Expose only the leg the candidate will be placing the stocking on.

steps.text: Turn stocking inside out to heel
steps.detailedText: Gather supplies and turn the stocking inside out to the heel.

steps.text: Place stocking over toes and heel
steps.detailedText: Place the stocking over the patient's toes, foot, and heel.
Gently pull the stocking up their leg.
Move foot and leg gently and naturally, avoiding force and over-extension of limb and joints.
Adjust stocking; stocking should be wrinkle-free to the knee.

steps.text: Call light; bed low and locked
steps.detailedText: Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient. Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Turn stocking inside out to heel.

skills.title: Gives Modified Bed Bath (Face and One Arm, Hand and Underarm)
skills.pedagogicalReason: Largest task — the body itself. Sets the standard for privacy, temperature, and patient dignity.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Check water temperature for safety.

Fill the basin with warm water and place it on a flat surface with a barrier underneath. Have the patient check the water temperature by placing their hand in the basin or putting a wet washcloth on the back of their hand.
Raise the height of the bed to safe level.
Don clean, non-sterile gloves.
Remove gown from the patient and place in the designated hamper.
Cover patient with bath blanket or towel for privacy.
Beginning with eyes, using a damp washcloth without soap, clean the eyes from inner to outer aspect using different sides of the washcloth.
Wash the face using a damp washcloth without soaps using different sides of the washcloth.
Pat dry the face.
Proceed to the arm, expose one arm and place a dry towel underneath.
Gather wet washcloth and apply soap.
Clean the arm and pat dry.

steps.text: Wash hands, fingers, fingernails; pat dry
steps.detailedText: Wash hands, fingers, and fingernails and pat dry.
Wash and clean underarm with soap and pat dry.
Dispose of the gown and used linens into the linen bag or laundry hamper.
Assist the patient to put on a clean gown.
While wearing gloves, empty the equipment.
Rinse the equipment.
Dry the basin.
Return the equipment to storage.
Dispose of soiled linen in the designated laundry hamper.

Remove gloves by turning them inside out — grasp the outside of one glove at the wrist and peel it off, then slide the fingers of the ungloved hand under the remaining glove at the wrist and peel it off turning inside out. Dispose of both gloves in the appropriate waste container without touching the outer surface.
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Patient checks water temperature.

skills.title: Provides Mouth Care
skills.pedagogicalReason: Moves to the face and head after the bath. Natural clinical sequence — mouth care follows or accompanies bath.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

steps.text: Place all supplies on a barrier
steps.detailedText: Place all supplies on a barrier.
Don clean, non-sterile gloves.
If the patient is in bed, elevate the head of the bed if it is permissible per the care plan.
Cover the patient's chest with a towel to keep their clothing or gown clean.
Wet the toothbrush in the sink or in a cup of water.
Apply a small amount of toothpaste to the toothbrush.
Brush the patient's teeth, including the inner, outer, and chewing surfaces of all upper and lower teeth.
After each quadrant of the mouth (i.e., lower right, lower left, upper right, or upper left), allow the patient to rinse with water and spit into an emesis basin.
Clean the patient's tongue, being careful not to cause the patient to gag.
Assist the patient in rinsing their mouth.
Wipe the patient's mouth with the towel on their chest.

steps.text: Remove towel; place in linen bag
steps.detailedText: Remove the towel and place it in a linen bag.
Empty the emesis basin.
Rinse the emesis basin.
Dry the emesis basin.
Rinse the toothbrush.

Remove gloves by turning them inside out — grasp the outside of one glove at the wrist and peel it off, then slide the fingers of the ungloved hand under the remaining glove at the wrist and peel it off turning inside out.
Dispose of the gloves in an appropriate container.
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Barrier under supplies; not a full basin bath.

skills.title: Cleans Upper or Lower Denture
skills.pedagogicalReason: Extension of mouth care — same skill with an added device. Follows directly.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Don clean, non-sterile gloves.

steps.text: Place all supplies on a barrier
steps.detailedText: Place all supplies on a barrier.
Place a clothing protector on the patient.
Line the sink with a washcloth or paper towel.
Ask the patient to remove dentures and place them in the cup.
Handle the dentures carefully to avoid damage or contamination.
Wet the denture brush and apply denture toothpaste if available. Water alone is acceptable to clean dentures if toothpaste is not available.
Thoroughly brush the inner, outer, and chewing surfaces of each denture.
Rinse the dentures using clean, cool water and place them on a clean barrier or in an emesis basin.
Rinse the denture cup.
Place the dentures in a rinsed cup.
Put a denture cleansing tablet in the cup, if desired.
Rinse the equipment (denture brush and emesis basin).
Return the equipment to storage.
Discard the protective lining in an appropriate container.

Remove gloves by turning them inside out — grasp the outside of one glove at the wrist and peel it off, then slide the fingers of the ungloved hand under the remaining glove at the wrist and peel it off turning inside out. Dispose of both gloves in the appropriate waste container without touching the outer surface.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Line sink; rinse over basin.

skills.title: Provides Foot Care on One Foot
skills.pedagogicalReason: Bottom of the body. Mirrors the lower-extremity focus from PROM. Introduces skin integrity inspection.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

Fill the basin with warm water. Verify the temperature is safe and comfortable (approximately 105–110°F / 40–43°C) using a thermometer or by having the patient test with their hand or a wet washcloth placed on the back of their hand.
Don clean, non-sterile gloves.

Fill a foot basin with warm water and place it on a flat surface with a barrier underneath. Have the patient check the water temperature by placing their hand in the basin or putting a wet washcloth on the back of their hand.
Remove their socks.
Immerse their feet in warm water for 5 to 20 minutes.
Use water and a soapy washcloth.
Wash each foot and between the toes.
Rinse the entire foot with the wet washcloth, including between the toes.
Dry the foot thoroughly, including between the toes.
Ask the patient if they would like lotion. If applying lotion, wear gloves.
Massage the lotion over the foot but avoid applying any lotion between the toes.
Wipe off any excess lotion with a dry towel.
Replace the socks or preferred footwear.
While wearing gloves, empty the equipment.
Rinse the equipment.
Dry the basin.
Dispose of soiled linen in a laundry hamper.

Remove gloves by turning them inside out — grasp the outside of one glove at the wrist and peel it off, then slide the fingers of the ungloved hand under the remaining glove at the wrist and peel it off turning inside out. Dispose of both gloves in the appropriate waste container without touching the outer surface.
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Soak 5–20 min; no lotion between toes.

skills.title: Dresses Client with Affected (Weak) Right Arm
skills.pedagogicalReason: Last in personal care — requires positioning, affected-side awareness, and sequencing. Most cognitively complex personal care skill.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Raise the height of the bed to a safe level.
Keep the patient covered while removing their gown.

steps.text: Remove gown from left side first
steps.detailedText: Remove the gown from the left side first.
Place the used gown in a designated laundry hamper.
Ask the patient about their preferences for desired clothing.

steps.note: Affected side dressed first; unaffected side last.
steps.detailedText: Start dressing them on their right side first. Insert their hand through the sleeve of their shirt and grasp the hand of the patient to guide it through the sleeve.
Place their unaffected arm in the shirt sleeve, grasping the hand of the patient. Finish putting on their shirt by buttoning.
Return the patient to lying on their back.
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Dress weak/affected arm first.

skills.title: Feeds Client Who Cannot Feed Self
skills.pedagogicalReason: Patient must be clean, dressed, and upright. Mirrors the care shift routine — hygiene before feeding.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, and verify the patient's identity using two identifiers (name and date of birth or wristband ID).
Explain the procedure to the patient.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Verify the name on the diet card matches the patient.
Verify the diet, diet texture, and liquid consistency matches the diet card.
Position the patient in an upright position, at least 45 degrees.
Place a clothing protector on the patient if desired (e.g., a paper or cloth towel or a large napkin).

steps.text: Ask about mouth care before eating
steps.detailedText: Ask the patient if they would like mouth care before eating.

steps.text: Assist patient to clean their hands
steps.detailedText: Assist the patient to clean their hands before feeding using sanitizer or soapy and wet washcloths.

steps.text: Sit at eye level facing patient
steps.detailedText: Position self in a chair at eye level facing the patient.
Describe the foods and fluids being offered to the patient.

steps.text: Offer small amounts at reasonable rate
steps.detailedText: Offer small amounts of food at a reasonable rate.
Offer fluids frequently.

steps.text: Allow time to chew and swallow
steps.detailedText: Allow the patient time to chew and swallow.
Wipe the patient's face whenever necessary.

steps.text: Alternate foods and fluids until full
steps.detailedText: Continue to alternate foods and fluids until the patient indicates they are full.
Clean the patient's face and hands.

steps.text: Ask about mouth care after eating
steps.detailedText: Ask the patient if they would like mouth care.
Leave the patient with their head elevated at least 30 degrees.
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Identify then explain; verify diet card name, texture, liquids.

skills.title: Assists With Use of Bedpan
skills.pedagogicalReason: Most basic elimination assist — no indwelling device. Teaches privacy, positioning, and cleanup sequence.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Lower the head of the bed.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Don clean, non-sterile gloves.

steps.text: Turn patient; place barrier under buttocks
steps.detailedText: Turn the patient and place a barrier (e.g., a towel, waterproof soaker pad, disposable pad) under their buttocks.
Position the patient on the bedpan correctly. The deeper portion of the bedpan should be directed toward their toes, and the patient should be centered on the bedpan.

Remove gloves by turning them inside out — grasp the outside of one glove at the wrist and peel it off, then slide the fingers of the ungloved hand under the remaining glove at the wrist and peel it off turning inside out. Dispose of both gloves in the appropriate waste container without touching the outer surface. Then perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Raise the head of the bed to a comfortable level.
Cover the patient with linens or a bath blanket.
Leave toilet tissue or wipes within reach of the patient.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Wait nearby allowing for patient privacy.
When the patient signals, return and assists the patient to perform hand hygiene in bed.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Lower the head of the bed to a comfortable level.
Don clean, non-sterile gloves.
Gently remove the bed pan.
Discard the soiled linen in the designated laundry hamper.
Assist with perineal care.
Empty the bedpan into the toilet.
Rinse the equipment used and empty the rinse water into the toilet.

Remove gloves by turning them inside out — grasp the outside of one glove at the wrist and peel it off, then slide the fingers of the ungloved hand under the remaining glove at the wrist and peel it off turning inside out. Dispose of both gloves in the appropriate waste container without touching the outer surface.
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Five hand-hygiene points; wait for patient privacy.

skills.title: Provides Perineal Care (Peri-Care) for Female
skills.pedagogicalReason: Post-elimination hygiene step. Completes the loop: assist, clean, document.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

Fill the basin with warm water. Verify the temperature is safe and comfortable (approximately 105–110°F / 40–43°C) using a thermometer or by having the patient test with their hand or a wet washcloth placed on the back of their hand.
Raise one side rail of the bed after checking the patient's mobility and their preferred side to lie on.
Don clean, non-sterile gloves.
Raise the bed height if needed.

Fill the basin with warm water and place it on a flat surface with a barrier underneath. Have the patient check the water temperature by placing their hand in the basin or putting a wet washcloth on the back of their hand.
Turn the patient or raise their hips and place a barrier (a towel, waterproof soaker pad, disposable pad, etc.) under their buttocks.
Expose their perineum only.
Separate the labia.
Use water and a soapy washcloth.
Clean one side of the labia from top to bottom.

steps.text: Clean other labia side; clean portion
steps.detailedText: Using a clean portion of the first washcloth, clean the other side of the labia from top to bottom.

steps.text: Clean perineal area top to bottom
steps.detailedText: Using a clean portion of the first washcloth, clean the perineal area from top to bottom.

steps.text: Put first washcloth in linen bag
steps.detailedText: Put the first washcloth in the linen bag.
Using the second clean washcloth, rinse one side of the labia from top to bottom.

steps.text: Rinse other labia side; clean portion
steps.detailedText: Using a clean portion of the second washcloth, rinse the other side of the labia from top to bottom.

steps.text: Rinse perineal area top to bottom
steps.detailedText: Using a clean portion of the second washcloth, rinse the perineal area from top to bottom.

steps.text: Put second washcloth in linen bag
steps.detailedText: Put the second washcloth in the linen bag.
Pat dry.

steps.text: Turn patient; grasp raised side rail
steps.detailedText: Assist the patient to turn onto their side facing away from the candidate and ask the patient to hold onto the raised side rail.

steps.text: Apply water and soap; third washcloth
steps.detailedText: Using the third clean washcloth, apply water and soap.

steps.text: Clean one buttock side; wipe away
steps.detailedText: Using a clean portion of the third washcloth, clean one side of the buttock, wiping away from perineal area.

steps.text: Clean other buttock side; wipe away
steps.detailedText: Using a clean portion of the third washcloth, clean the other side of the buttock, wiping away from the perineal area.
Using a clean portion of the third washcloth, clean the rectal area wiping away from the perineal area.

steps.text: Put third washcloth in linen bag
steps.detailedText: Put the third washcloth in the linen bag.

steps.text: Rinse one buttock side; wipe away
steps.detailedText: Using the fourth washcloth, rinse one side of the buttock wiping away from the perineal area.

steps.text: Rinse other buttock side; wipe away
steps.detailedText: Using a clean portion of the fourth washcloth, rinse the other side of the buttock wiping away from the perineal area.
Using a clean portion of the fourth washcloth, rinse the rectal area wiping away from perineal area.

steps.text: Put fourth washcloth in linen bag
steps.detailedText: Put the fourth washcloth in the linen bag.
Pat dry.
Safely remove the waterproof pad from under the buttocks.

Remove gloves by turning them inside out — grasp the outside of one glove at the wrist and peel it off, then slide the fingers of the ungloved hand under the remaining glove at the wrist and peel it off turning inside out. Dispose of both gloves in the appropriate waste container without touching the outer surface.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
Position the patient on her back.
Don clean, non-sterile gloves.
Dispose of soiled linen in the designated laundry hamper.
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Longest skill; front-to-back; wipe away from perineum.

skills.title: Provides Catheter Care for Female
skills.pedagogicalReason: Last skill in the entire list. Requires infection control, perineal anatomy, and documentation. Most invasive and highest-consequence skill.

steps.detailedText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

Fill the basin with warm water. Verify the temperature is safe and comfortable (approximately 105–110°F / 40–43°C) using a thermometer or by having the patient test with their hand or a wet washcloth placed on the back of their hand.
Don clean, non-sterile gloves.

Fill the basin with warm water and place it on a flat surface with a barrier underneath. Have the patient check the water temperature by placing their hand in the basin or putting a wet washcloth on the back of their hand.
Raise the bed height to a working height.
Expose only the urethra and catheter.
Follow the tubing from the patient toward the drainage bag, ensuring that the tubing is at a lower level as it goes toward the bag. Be sure no kinks or elevation can cause backflow to the bladder.
Turn the patient or raise their hips and place a barrier (e.g., a towel, waterproof soaker pad, or disposable pad) under their buttocks.
Use the first washcloth with soap and water to carefully wash around the catheter where it exits the urethra.
Hold the catheter where it exits the urethra with one hand.
While holding the catheter, clean 4 inches down the catheter tube.
Clean with strokes moving away from the urethra.

steps.text: Use clean washcloth portion each stroke
steps.detailedText: Use a clean portion of washcloth for each stroke.

steps.text: Put first washcloth in linen bag
steps.detailedText: Put the soiled first washcloth in the linen bag.
Wet the second washcloth and rinse, using strokes only away from the urethra while continuing to hold the catheter where it exits the urethra.
Rinse using a clean portion of washcloth for each stroke.

steps.text: Put second washcloth in linen bag
steps.detailedText: Put the soiled second washcloth in the linen bag.
Pat dry with a towel.
Do not allow the tube to be pulled at any time during the procedure.
Replace the gown over the patient's perineal area.
While wearing gloves, empty the basin.
Rinse the basin.
Dry the basin.
Return the equipment to storage.
Dispose of soiled linen in a designated laundry hamper.

Remove gloves by turning them inside out — grasp the outside of one glove at the wrist and peel it off, then slide the fingers of the ungloved hand under the remaining glove at the wrist and peel it off turning inside out. Dispose of both gloves in the appropriate waste container without touching the outer surface.
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

skills.studentFocus: Hold catheter at urethra; strokes away; tubing downhill; never pull.
